rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL structure and insert annex content only within the owning component or implementation scope.
Keep type-level content limited to features visible at that type; implementation-only references belong only in implementation scope when valid there.
Do not introduce references that cross scope boundaries or depend on subcomponent paths unless the owner scope permits them.
generic_rules: Choose the owner scope so references are legal there and architecture elements remain unchanged.
Preserve existing subcomponents, connections, features, properties, and ordering unless a scoped annex insertion requires minimal local edits.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Implementation-level bindings belong after the relevant structural declarations in the implementation scope.
If type-level statements are needed, they may reference only identifiers visible from that component type interface.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-only references in a component type-owned annex.
Do not alter architecture structure or move unrelated declarations just to accommodate inserted contract content.
irrelevant_topics: Clause semantics for specific input/output relationships
Helper expression patterns for deterministic output computation
Scalar AGREE type-name details unless validator diagnostics require syntax repair
